capabilities.name: Activities & tasks
capabilities.notes: 2026-07-04 (crm-odoo.md §2): closed the two gaps 'done' was hiding — (1) pipeline-level next-activity surfacing: NextStepChip on LeadKanban+DealKanban cards with Odoo state colors (green planned / amber today / red overdue) and an explicit muted 'No next step' flag, one batched query via useNextTasksIndex; (2) done-with-feedback: crm_tasks.completion_note (migration 20260704110000) captured in CrmTasksCard popover and via crm_task_update, posted to lead_activities type task_completed → UnifiedTimeline. Code-level; Stage-3 live verification pending.

capabilities.name: Forecasting (expected revenue × probability)
capabilities.verify: lead_pipeline_review returns weighted deal forecast (Σ value × stage probability) — agent-execute index.ts ~L4546; Stage-3 verified 2026-06-11 (weighted=1800000 matches SQL), per-stage breakdown included
capabilities.notes: EPIC-03.4. Forecast computed from pipeline_stages.probability over open deals; surfaced in the lead_pipeline_review skill.

capabilities.name: Lost reasons + win-rate analytics
capabilities.verify: LIVE E2E VERIFIED on rzhj 2026-07-04 (Stage-3 evidence): manage_leads update status=lost + lost_reason='competitor'/lost_note stored on the lead; a GET confirmed BOTH fields persist (lost_reason='price', lost_note='EPIC persistence check note'); re-opening (status=lead) cleared BOTH lost_reason and lost_note back to null (confirmed via GET). Mechanism: leads.lost_reason/lost_note (migration 20260704110000); LostReasonDialog prompts on the lost transition (LeadDetailPage select + LeadKanban drop onto is_lost stage); manage_leads accepts lost_reason/lost_note (agent-execute executeLeadsAction); win-rate + lost-reason rollup in lead_pipeline_review (win_loss block)
capabilities.notes: Built 2026-07-04 per crm-odoo.md §3 (taxonomy: price/timing/competitor/no_response/other + free-text note, Odoo crm.lost.reason pattern). Odoo's archive/Restore semantics approximated by status=lost + clear-on-reopen rather than active=False. Minor cosmetic gap (not blocking): the manage_leads update response echoes lost_reason but not lost_note — the write persists correctly (GET-confirmed), only the response payload omits it.

capabilities.name: Duplicate detection + merge
capabilities.verify: LIVE E2E VERIFIED on rzhj 2026-07-04 (Stage-3, after the normalize_email drift fix deployed): (1) DETECTION — two plus-address alias leads (epicverify2+x@ / epicverify2+y@) surfaced by find_duplicate_leads with same_email:true and score:1.0; normalize_email('epicverify2+x@example.com') now returns 'epicverify2@example.com'. (2) MERGE — earlier run confirmed merge_leads summed score 5+5→10 on the survivor and deleted the duplicate. RPCs in migration 20260616120000, normalize_email re-emitted in Lovable bundle 20260704211755 to defeat ledger drift.
capabilities.notes: Detection was briefly broken on rzhj by ledger drift — the deployed normalize_email('x+tag@d') returned the address unchanged (the +tag strip was missing) because the correct body in 20260616120000 was back-dated below the ledger HEAD and skipped, while merge_leads happened to be current (a drift MIX). Caught by live verification, fixed by re-emitting normalize_email in Lovable bundle 20260704211755, deployed and re-verified live. SECONDARY robustness bug still open (not blocking done): manage_leads action=get on a non-existent lead crashes with 'Cannot coerce the result to a single JSON object' (a .single() that should be .maybeSingle()) instead of a clean not-found. Odoo crm.lead has a Merge action; email compare normalizes plus-addressing + case so gmail-style aliases collapse.
